A.text: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.

B.text: Each hexagon is adjacent only to pentagons.

C.text: No two hexagons share a common edge.

D.text: Two pentagons share a common edge.

parsed.answer_value: No two hexagons share a common edge.
parsed.rationale: The image shows a spherical molecular structure composed of interconnected polygons, resembling a fullerene. In such structures, the fundamental rule is that no two hexagons can share an edge; they are always separated by pentagons. This arrangement allows the structure to curve and close into a sphere. Observing the connections in the diagram confirms this pattern, as every hexagon is surrounded by pentagons. Therefore, the correct observation is that no two hexagons share a common edge.
